marketState: neutral-bearish — weather premium fully priced, seasonal and storage headwinds building
summary: Natural gas (Aug front) is trading at $3.317, roughly $0.36 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 and in the upper half of the $2.46–$3.46 band (status: SLIGHTLY OVER on all four forward horizons). Weather demand remains far above the 5-year maximum for both the 1–7D (pctVs5y +46%, rangePos 1.85) and 8–16D (pctVs5y +30%, rangePos 1.32) windows — the key near-term bull signal — but the 24-hour trend has flipped from accelerating to slightly declining (-0.9 Bcf/d), suggesting the heat event may be peaking. Beyond the near term, the case for lower prices strengthens: storage is +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise, the 4-week seasonal win-rate is only 19% (typical move -1.8%), and fair value over the next three weeks stays in the $2.95–$3.12 range — well below spot. Managed-money positioning at the 18th 5-year percentile (net short -82,722) remains the key contrarian wildcard that constrains short conviction.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality assigns only 19% probability of price being higher in 4 weeks with a typical 4-week move of -1.8% and low reliability; the fair-value model shows front is 'SLIGHTLY OVER' on all horizons through +21 days with the gap only narrowing from $0.357 to $0.196 as fair value itself rises slowly to $3.12 — price is not reverting to fair value on its own. Storage surplus (+151 Bcf vs 5-year average, bearish surprise) and the bearish 50/200 EMA cross on both the daily and weekly timeframes reinforce the downward structural bias once the weather premium fades.
longTerm.invalidation: Weekly close above $3.50 (above the current fair-value band top of $3.46), or storage deficit vs 5-year average flips negative, or weather demand remains above the 5-year maximum continuously beyond mid-July compressing storage.

shortTerm.rationale: Demand is still above the 5-year historical maximum in both near windows, making a new short high-risk; but the 24-hour demand trend has flipped to -0.9 Bcf/d (vs +8.5 in the prior snapshot) and the 1-hour RSI is overbought at 75.8, suggesting the weather-driven spike may be exhausting near the band top. Entering long here chases an already-extended move with the weather catalyst likely peaking; entering short still risks a squeeze if heat holds.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter SHORT if price reaches $3.44–$3.46 (fair-value band top) with 1-hour RSI still above 70, confirming exhaustion at resistance; OR if weather demand model revisions drop the 1–7 day pctVs5y below +20% (demand rolling back toward historical range), signalling the heat-driven premium is fading — target $3.10–$3.15 with stop above $3.50.

keyDrivers: Weather demand above 5-year historical max in both 1–7D (rangePos 1.85, +46% vs 5y) and 8–16D (rangePos 1.32, +30% vs 5y) windows — primary near-term bull catalyst, though 24h trend has turned -0.9 Bcf/d
Storage surplus +151 Bcf vs 5-year average with bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf above forecast) — structural medium-term bear headwind

risks: Persistent extreme heat holds demand above 5-year maximum through late July, sustaining the fair-value premium and squeezing shorts toward or above the band top ($3.46–$3.52)
COT at 18th percentile net-short means a sustained bull catalyst could trigger a sharp short-covering rally well above $3.50, requiring a full reassessment of the short thesis
